remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Dataiku named a Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for 5th consecutive year
remarks.content: For the fifth consecutive year, Dataiku has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for AI Platforms for Data Science and Machine Learning, once again recognized in the Leader quadrant for both “Completeness of Vision” and “Ability to Execute.”
remarks.contentTranslation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为2026年Gartner ®数据科学和机器学习AI平台魔力象限™的领导者，再次在“愿景的完整性”和“执行能力”的领导者象限中获得认可。
remarks.translation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为Gartner魔力象限领导者
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Lilian Weng's Blog (OpenAI)
remarks.role: AI research deep-dives, LLM architectures, agent systems, multimodal, alignment, safety.
remarks.text: Scaling Laws, Carefully
remarks.content: Scaling laws are one of the most critical empirical findings in deep learning. The observation is simple in form: the training loss $L$ decreases predictably as we scale up model size $N$, dataset size $D$, and compute $C$, following a power-law curve, which appears as a straight line on a log-log p
remarks.contentTranslation: 缩放定律是深度学习中最关键的经验发现之一。观察结果的形式很简单：当我们按照幂律曲线（在对数对数p上显示为直线）按比例放大模型大小$ N $、数据集大小$ D $和计算$ C $时，训练损失$ L $可预测地减少
remarks.translation: 谨慎地扩展法律
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: AI explainability in finance: auditable models, GenAI, and agents
remarks.content: Regulators are asking financial institutions a direct question: Can you explain how your AI made that decision? For most, the honest answer is no — not fully, not on demand, and not across every system that now influences regulated decisions. Compliance functions are expected to cover
remarks.contentTranslation: 监管机构正在向金融机构提出一个直接的问题：你能解释一下你的AI是如何做出这个决定的吗？对于大多数人来说，诚实的答案是否定的—不是完全的，不是按需提供的，也不是每个现在影响受监管决策的系统。合规职能预计将涵盖
remarks.translation: 财务中的AI可解释性：可审计模型、GenAI和Agent商
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: The AI success gap: why more AI doesn’t add up to more value
remarks.content: If AI is everywhere, why are so few companies getting real value from it?That is the question most enterprises are now facing. Eighty-eight percent of companies use AI, but only six percent generate meaningful value from it, according to a study from McKinsey.Pilots run, demos impress, a
remarks.contentTranslation: 如果AI无处不在，为什么很少有公司从中获得真正的价值？这是大多数企业现在面临的问题。根据麦肯锡的一项研究，百分之八十八的公司使用AI，但只有百分之六的公司从中产生有意义的价值。
remarks.translation: AI成功差距：为什么更多的AI不能带来更多的价值

remarks.text:
minor milestone in the growth of swyx inc:

we took over my new media lab today

it will be the new home for engineer-creatives in san francisco; a third place to make; a finishing school for technical storytellers; a place to inspire the inspirational.

to our complete surprise; it came with a datacenter rack randomly set up and wired up! need advice on what to put in this thing. @alexocheema halp
remarks.translation: swyx inc发展的一个小里程碑：我们今天接管了我的新媒体实验室，它将成为旧金山工程师-创意人员的新家；第三个工作场所；技术讲故事者的整理学校；一个激发灵感的地方。令我们完全惊讶的是；它配备了一个随机设置和连接的数据中心机架！需要有关在这个东西里放什么的建议。@ alexocheema halp

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Agents are particularly hard-to-debug software. 

For one, and by design, AI models behave in non-deterministic ways. Even two identical prompts don't always yield the same output.

But agents are also complex distributed systems. They involve multiple steps of computation across functions and sandboxes, touching dozens of API services that can go down, rate limit you, etc.

Nailing down observability out of the box for https://t.co/nDDXqUmOlD on Vercel was a key priority for the team, and the feedback so far has been 🔥↓
remarks.translation:
Agent是特别难以调试的软件。首先，从设计上讲，AI模型的行为方式是不确定的。即使是两个相同的提示也不总是产生相同的输出。但智能体也是复杂的分布式系统。它们涉及跨函数和沙箱的多个计算步骤，涉及数十个可能发生故障、速率限制的API服务等。
在Vercel上开箱即用地确定可观察性是团队的关键优先事项，到目前为止，反馈一直是 🔥↓
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
We are giving all Codex users a usage reset on the house. Should be showing in your accounts in the next few hours.

We have applied some mitigations, but our investigation hasn't shown users being impacted at large. We are continuing to monitor the situation. https://t.co/rLJrQdI1ks
remarks.translation: 我们正在为所有Codex用户提供房屋使用情况重置。将在接下来的几个小时内显示在您的账号中。我们已经采取了一些缓解措施，但我们的调查并未显示用户普遍受到影响。我们正在继续监控情况。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Now that the dust is settled, what’s really funny to me about the taste commentary is it’s coming from a LOT of people who never have built anything..

You can’t achieve (or refine) taste without being in the arena. 

It’s like a chef cooking and expecting their first dish to be good on the first go. 

But, when you give it a 100 iterations and maybe cook a 100 dishes, then the 101st one has imbued all the lessons from those 10,000 iterations. 

But, if you over fit without much variation, then people get tired of it since well they want something new as well. 

The best people are able to learn, imbue and innovate by breaking patterns consistently. 

It’ll be interesting to see how AI does the same - and unlike most people I think it has a fair shot at actually building taste. 

End rant.
remarks.translation:
现在尘埃落定，对我来说，味道评论真正有趣的是，它来自许多从未建造过任何东西的人。没有在舞台上，你无法实现（或完善）味道。这就像厨师在烹饪，并期望他们的第一道菜在第一道菜上就很好。但是，当你给它100次迭代并可能烹饪100道菜肴时，那么第101道菜已经灌输了这10000次迭代的所有课程。
但是，如果您过度适合而没有太多变化，那么人们会厌倦它，因为他们也想要新的东西。最优秀的人能够通过不断打破模式来学习、灌输和创新。看看AI如何做到这一点会很有趣--与大多数人不同，我认为它在实际营造品味方面有公平的机会。结束咆哮。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
So let me get this straight:

1. We publish frontier models
2. They get distilled into cheap open source models
3. US companies adopt the same open source models because they're good enough and much cheaper
4. We start gating access to frontier models

What next? US companies innovate less? Open source models become more attractive?
remarks.translation: 让我直截了当地说： 1.我们发布前沿模型2.它们被提炼成廉价的开源模型3.美国公司采用相同的开源模式，因为它们足够好，而且便宜得多4。我们开始控制对前沿车型的访问接下来该怎么做？美国公司创新较少？开源模型变得更具吸引力？
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.text:
An interesting side effect of AI is that I have zero tolerance for shallow interactions with humans.

I really deep connection and depth in my relationships. More ways to do that are so valuable.

And for everything else: give me agents.

I suspect our world will become both smaller and more rich in it's relationship depth.
remarks.translation: AI的一个有趣的副作用是，我对与人类的浅层互动持零容忍态度。我在人际关系中真正深刻的联系和深度。更多的方法是非常有价值的。对于其他一切：给我Agent商。我怀疑我们的世界将变得更小，更丰富的关系深度。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

we have been scaling without slop by working with aligned domain experts to add coverage

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
From what I'm seeing, alot of the money has moved to services (with some software bundled), not software.

People want outcomes, not tools. 

It's feels really hard to build a pure-play software company that's more valuable to people or companies than just using Codex/Claude Code with a bunch of personal skills and agents.

Thoughts?
remarks.translation: 从我所看到的情况来看，很多钱已经转移到服务（捆绑了一些软件） ，而不是软件。人们想要的是结果，而不是工具。要建立一个对人或公司更有价值的纯软件公司，而不仅仅是使用具有一堆个人技能和Agent的Codex/Claude Code ，感觉真的很难。想法？

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dan Shipper
remarks.role: ceo every
remarks.text:
BREAKING: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 Sol!

As of today, by U.S. government directive, access is limited to only ~20 pre-approved companies and @every is not on the list. This appears to be a temporary situation while the government races to figure out a long-term policy for releasing frontier models with advanced capabilities.

I understand and applaud the need for some government oversight in making American infrastructure resilient to cyberattacks and other potential new threats from the misuse of these models. However, I also strongly believe that widespread democratic access to frontier models is absolutely necessary to our country’s leading position in the AI race. It’s also critical for allowing American workers to keep pace with the new skills they need to be productive in this era.

A world where advanced models are locked up only for use by the employees of AI giants and a select few companies is one where ambitious students, independent builders, and working professionals are denied the tools they need to learn, create, and compete to their fullest potential.

Also, speaking for @every and the community of developers and writers who share early access with us: Our job is to test these tools early so that we can prepare Americans for how to use them to their fullest extent. If we lose access, we lose the ability to do that important job well.

Thankfully, both OpenAI and the government seem to be working to make broad access available soon.

We’ll be ready to vibe check when that happens :)
remarks.translation:
突破： OpenAI宣布GPT-5.6 Sol ！截至今天，根据美国政府的指令，访问仅限于约20家预先批准的公司， @ every不在名单上。这似乎是一种暂时的情况，因为政府正在竞相制定一项长期政策，以发布具有先进功能的前沿车型。
我理解并赞扬政府有必要进行一些监督，使美国基础设施能够抵御网络攻击以及滥用这些模型带来的其他潜在新威胁。然而，我也坚信，广泛民主地进入前沿模式对于我们国家在AI竞赛中的领先地位是绝对必要的。这对于让美国工人跟上他们在这个时代提高生产力所需的新技能也至关重要。
在一个只有AI巨头和少数几家公司的员工才能使用先进模型的世界里，雄心勃勃的学生、独立建筑商和工作专业人士被剥夺了他们学习、创造和最大限度地发挥潜力所需的工具。
此外，代表与我们共享抢先体验的开发人员和作家社区：我们的工作是尽早测试这些工具，以便我们为美国人如何最大限度地使用它们做好准备。如果我们失去了访问权限，我们就失去了很好地完成这项重要工作的能力。值得庆幸的是， OpenAI和政府似乎都在努力在不久的将来提供广泛的访问权限。如果发生这种情况，我们将随时为您提供帮助:)
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
Small things I wish Claude Code had:

1. Bring back ability to steer conversations while Claude is working

2. Make mobile remote control for all threads on by default

3. The shortcut keys seem only accessible if you have the sub-menu open? Consider supporting "cmd + key" so we can hotkey to different threads with the keyboard. If I hold down cmd I should see all the shortcuts in the UI.

4. Let me drag and drop to re-arrange my projects on left nav
remarks.translation: 我希望Claude Code拥有的小东西： 1.在Claude工作期间恢复引导对话的能力2.默认情况下，对所有线程进行移动远程控制3.只有在打开子菜单的情况下，才能访问快捷键？考虑支持“cmd + key” ，这样我们就可以使用键盘热键连接到不同的线程。如果我按住cmd ，我应该会看到UI中的所有快捷方式。4.让我在左侧导航栏上拖放以重新排列我的项目
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。
